Initial release: China job site scraping guide with battle-tested scripts

Comprehensive guide documenting 7 major technical challenges encountered
while scraping Chinese job platforms (Boss直聘, 飞书招聘, 字节跳动, 腾讯 etc.)
and their solutions after 8 rounds of iteration.

Includes:
- SPA rendering with Playwright
- Anti-bot bypass via CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol)
- AppleScript fallback for zero-detection scraping (macOS)
- Feishu recruitment portal specialized extractor
- LLM agent hallucination prevention patterns
- Multi-selector DOM extraction with fallbacks

Fix Post SF1/SF2 assignment type mapping to OR (#54)

Post SF1 and Post SF2 in ASSIGNMENT_OPTIONS were incorrectly mapped to
type SF1/SF2, making them pager-eligible and creating phantom pager
shifts. They should be type OR (general) since post-call days are
regular OR assignments — matching both the calendar sync auto-mapper
and the QGenda import logic.

Co-authored-by: Riley Crandall <rileycrandall04@users.noreply.github.com>

new ones. Add dev-stop target that cleans up from PID files and also
kills anything on ports 8080/7331 as a fallback.

commit.message:
Only hash messages that were actually compressed away

The compressor keeps recent messages verbatim in the prefix. Only store
hashes for the messages that got summarized — not the recent ones that
stay verbatim. This fixes hash mismatches caused by Claude Code modifying
the latest assistant response after streaming.

commit.message:
fix: remove --bundles args to allow updater artifact generation

In Tauri v2, 'updater' is not a valid bundle type. Updater artifacts
(.app.tar.gz.sig, .nsis.zip.sig) are auto-generated when
TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY is set, but only with default bundle types.
Passing --bundles dmg,updater caused only dmg to build, skipping the
app bundle that triggers updater generation.

publish-updater job now downloads sigs directly from release assets.

- Added explicit error logging for SystemSettings.set() failures

commit.message:
Add real 1.1.4 nsys profiling results from HPC

All results from actual nsys profiling on A100-SXM4-40GB:
- (a) nsys matches timeit within 0.1ms
- (b) ampere_sgemm_* dominates at 70-94% of GPU time
- (c) elementwise/reduce/sigmoid kernels fill remaining 15-22%
- (d) Matmul drops from 78% to 64% in full training step
- (e) Softmax ~1-2% vs matmul 79-85% of GPU kernel time

commit.message:
fix: 修復編輯活動時報名資料被連鎖刪除的嚴重 Bug

根本原因：upsertTicketTypes() 會先 DELETE ALL ticket_types 再 INSERT，
而 registrations 表有 ON DELETE CASCADE，導致所有報名資料被連鎖刪除。

修復方式：
- EventForm 載入票種時保留原始 id
- upsertTicketTypes() 改為真正的 upsert：
  1. 只刪除被使用者明確移除的票種
  2. 現有票種 UPDATE in place（保留 id，不觸發 cascade）
  3. 只 INSERT 全新的票種

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: DAVE E2EE voice encryption support + discordgo fork API compatibility (#71)

* fix: discordgo API compatibility fixes for MohmmedAshraf fork Dec 2025

* fix: DAVE E2EE voice support via godave/libdave + ubuntu:24.04 runtime base

- Add disgoorg/godave + golibdave for full MLS handshake (opcodes 21-24)
- Add local discordgo-dave/ fork with DAVE opcode handling in voice.go
- Add discord/dave.go adapter bridging discordgo DaveSession interface to godave
- Update Dockerfile runtime to ubuntu:24.04 (glibc 2.38+ required by libdave.so)
- Update docker-restart.sh to mount DAVE binary/libdave from dave-binary/ dir
- Fix discordgo API changes: Disconnect() ctx removal, vc.Ready bool

Resolves Discord close 4017 (E2EE/DAVE protocol required, enforced March 2 2026)

commit.message:
Add safety guardrails (Phase 4)

New module: guardrails.py with three safety layers:

1. Shell command safety:
   - Block destructive commands (rm -rf /, mkfs, fork bombs)
   - Warn on dangerous commands (sudo, force push, DROP TABLE)

2. File access control:
   - ADKCODE_ALLOWED_DIRS restricts agent to specific directories
   - Blocks reading .env files (may contain secrets)
   - Blocks access to /etc/shadow, /etc/passwd, /etc/sudoers

3. Audit log:
   - ADKCODE_AUDIT_LOG enables JSON lines logging of all tool calls
   - Sanitizes sensitive data (passwords, tokens, large content)

All file tools (read, write, edit) and shell tool now check
guardrails before executing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
